private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-14

**Source failures:** None observed.

## Worth attention

- **Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/13/us-government-directive-to-suspend-access/#atom-everything
  The US government issued an export control directive suspending access to Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the US. This is unprecedented — a government mandating access restrictions on specific AI models. If you rely on these models or serve international users, you need fallback plans immediately.

- **Program Claude Code, Codex, Pi and other agent harnesses with AI SDK** — https://vercel.com/changelog/program-agent-harnesses-with-ai-sdk
  AI SDK 7 introduces HarnessAgent, a single API for running established agent harnesses including Claude Code, Codex, and Pi. Write the agent once, swap harnesses via config. Reduces lock-in if you're building agent orchestration.

- **21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg** — https://depthfirst.com/research/21-zero-days-in-ffmpeg
  Security researchers disclosed 21 zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg across multiple codecs and formats. If you use FFmpeg anywhere in your stack, check your version and patch.

- **Looking Forward to Postgres 19: It's About Time** — https://www.pgedge.com/blog/looking-forward-to-postgres-19-its-about-time
  Preview of Postgres 19 features with focus on time-related improvements. Worth tracking for schema design decisions.

- **WASI 0.3 Launched** — https://bytecodealliance.org/articles/WASI-0.3
  WASI 0.3 ships async support and component model integration for WebAssembly. Makes portable server-side Wasm more practical.

- **Kimi K2.7 Code now available on AI Gateway** — https://vercel.com/changelog/kimi-k2-7-code-now-available-on-ai-gateway
  Moonshot AI's K2.7 Code is a multimodal coding model for long-horizon tasks, now on Vercel AI Gateway. Another option if evaluating coding models.

- **OpenAI WebRTC Audio Session, now with document context** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/12/openai-webrtc/#atom-everything
  Simon Willison's WebRTC audio tool now supports document context in realtime voice sessions. Useful reference for voice interfaces that reason over documents.

- **How to setup a local coding agent on macOS** — https://ikyle.me/blog/2026/how-to-setup-a-local-coding-agent-on-macos
  Guide for setting up local coding agents on macOS with local models. Relevant if running Ollama-based coding setups.

- **Slightly reducing the sloppiness of AI generated front end** — https://envs.net/~volpe/blog/posts/reduce-slop.html
  Practical tips for reducing common quality issues in AI-generated frontend code.

## Full digest
- [P] [simon-willison] Statement on US govt directive to suspend Fable 5/Mythos 5 — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/13/us-government-directive-to-suspend-access/#atom-everything — Export control directive suspends models for foreign nationals

results.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
results.summary: The US government issued an export control directive suspending access to Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the US. This is unprecedented — a government mandating access restrictions on specific AI models. If you rely on these models or serve international users, you need fallback plans immediately. The directive's scope and duration remain unclear.
results.raw_claim: US government issued export control directive suspending Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access for foreign nationals
results.uncertainty: Duration and exact legal scope of the directive are not yet clear
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly impacts anyone using Claude's frontier models or serving international users
results.verdict_reason: Major policy change directly affecting Claude model access

results.title: Program Claude Code, Codex, Pi and other agent harnesses with AI SDK
results.summary: Vercel's AI SDK 7 introduces HarnessAgent, a unified API to swap between agent harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) without rewriting code. Write the agent once and switch harnesses via config. Relevant if you're building agent orchestration — reduces lock-in to any single harness.
results.raw_claim: AI SDK 7 introduces HarnessAgent for swapping between Claude Code, Codex, and Pi harnesses via unified API
results.uncertainty: Experimental release; adapter coverage may be limited initially
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Practical tool for reducing agent harness lock-in
results.verdict_reason: New abstraction layer for agent harnesses with practical lock-in reduction

results.title: 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg
results.summary: Security researchers disclosed 21 zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the ubiquitous media processing library. If you use FFmpeg in any pipeline (video transcoding, media processing, thumbnail generation), check your version and update. The vulnerabilities span multiple codecs and formats.
results.raw_claim: 21 zero-day vulnerabilities disclosed in FFmpeg across multiple codecs and formats
results.uncertainty: Patch status and affected version ranges need verification
results.worth_mentioning_reason: FFmpeg is ubiquitous; 21 zero-days is a significant security event
results.verdict_reason: Significant security disclosure in widely-used library

results.title: Looking Forward to Postgres 19: It's About Time
results.summary: Preview of Postgres 19 features, including improvements to time-related functionality. Postgres 19 is expected to ship later this year. Worth tracking if you're on Postgres — new features may influence schema design or query patterns.
results.raw_claim: Postgres 19 preview highlights time-related improvements and new features coming later this year
results.uncertainty: Feature list may change before final release
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Postgres is core infrastructure for many solo devs
results.verdict_reason: Practical infrastructure preview for Postgres users

results.title: WASI 0.3 Launched
results.summary: WASI 0.3 has launched, bringing async support and component model integration to WebAssembly's system interface. This is a significant milestone for portable server-side Wasm — makes it practical to build composable services that run anywhere.
results.raw_claim: WASI 0.3 launched with async support and component model integration for WebAssembly
results.uncertainty: Ecosystem tooling and runtime support may still be catching up
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Major Wasm milestone that makes server-side Wasm more practical
results.verdict_reason: Significant infrastructure milestone for WebAssembly ecosystem

results.title: Kimi K2.7 Code now available on AI Gateway
results.summary: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code is now available on Vercel AI Gateway. It's a coding model designed for long-horizon programming tasks with native multimodal support (text + vision). Always runs in thinking mode. Another option if you're evaluating coding model alternatives.
results.raw_claim: Kimi K2.7 Code from Moonshot AI available on Vercel AI Gateway for long-horizon coding tasks
results.worth_mentioning_reason: New coding model option with multimodal support
results.verdict_reason: Narrower scope — one more coding model option

results.title: OpenAI WebRTC Audio Session, now with document context
results.summary: Simon Willison updated his OpenAI WebRTC audio tool to support document context — you can now feed documents into a realtime audio session. Useful reference if you're building voice interfaces that need to reason over documents.
results.raw_claim: OpenAI WebRTC audio session tool now supports feeding document context into realtime voice conversations
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Relevant to voice agent development patterns
results.verdict_reason: Useful reference for voice agent builders but narrower audience

results.title: How to setup a local coding agent on macOS
results.summary: A guide on setting up local coding agents on macOS, covering local model options and agent harness configuration. Directly relevant if you're running Ollama-based coding agents locally.
results.raw_claim: Guide covers setting up local coding agents on macOS with local model options
results.uncertainty: Quality and depth of guide not verified
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly relevant to local agent development on Mac
results.verdict_reason: Useful how-to but may retread known ground

results.title: Slightly reducing the sloppiness of AI generated front end
results.summary: Practical tips for reducing the common sloppiness in AI-generated frontend code — CSS alignment issues, inconsistent spacing, etc. Useful workflow advice if you're shipping AI-assisted UI.
results.raw_claim: Practical techniques for reducing common quality issues in AI-generated frontend code
results.uncertainty: Techniques may be specific to certain AI tools
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Practical workflow improvement for AI-assisted frontend dev
results.verdict_reason: Useful practical tips but narrow scope

results.title: H.R. 6028 would fundamentally change the U.S. Copyright Office
results.summary: EFF reports on H.R. 6028, which would restructure the US Copyright Office. Potential implications for AI and software copyright issues worth tracking.
results.raw_claim: H.R. 6028 would restructure the US Copyright Office with potential implications for AI and software
results.uncertainty: Bill status and final form unclear
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Potential policy impact on AI/software copyright
results.verdict_reason: Policy development worth tracking but no immediate action

results.title: Malware developers added nuclear and biological weapons text to their spyware
results.summary: Report of malware packages on npm embedding nuclear/biological weapons text, likely to trigger government monitoring systems and create noise. Supply chain security reminder.
results.raw_claim: Malware developers embedding nuclear and biological weapons text in npm spyware packages
results.uncertainty: Scale and specific packages affected unclear
results.verdict_reason: Supply chain security signal worth tracking

results.title: erm: A Local CLI That Strips Ums, Uhs, and Erms From Speech
results.summary: Local CLI tool that strips filler words (um, uh, erm) from speech recordings using local processing. Potentially useful for podcast/video post-production.
results.raw_claim: Local CLI tool strips filler words from speech recordings using local processing
results.uncertainty: Quality of filler word detection not verified
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Useful local tool for content creators
results.verdict_reason: Useful tool but not decision-changing
